bio:
- counseling in a variety of settings since 2014. 
- Master of Arts in professional counseling from Lancaster Bible College in 2014
- 

third person 
My experience in both counseling and organizational leadership led me to begin Morning Light Counseling as the specific need for the provision of counseling services for men became evident. My passion and focus areas include providing Spiritually-integrated counseling for men struggling with anxiety, depression, anger, addictive behavior, relationship issues, parenting issues, and motivation concerns.



Jon is honored to walk with individuals, couples, and families through the issues of life. He is distinctively Christian and person-centered. Clients are empowered to partner with Jon in the therapeutic process. Biblical references and prayer are utilized according to the comfort level of each client.

Jon has worked extensively with persons seeking restoration and hope in marriage and family issues, depression, anxiety, and abuse. He has also worked with drug and sex addictions. Jon’s foundational belief for counseling is that God is the author and only true provider of restoration and hope. His desire is that you will encounter God through Jesus Christ so that you may walk in confidence born from the reality that He made you, delights in you, and has great plans to use you for His glory.
approachBody:
I am a firm and passionate believer in the importance of staying anchored to Gospel truth. As such, my counsel flows out of that belief. My approach blends narrative work with the narrative of the Biblical story.

Reach out!
credentials: Licensed Professional Counselor (LPC), Commonwealth of Pennsylvania
Focused practice: counseling for men, ages 12 and up
quote: If you want to build a ship, don't drum up the men to gather wood, divide the work and give orders. Instead, teach them to yearn for the vast and endless sea.
